news.title: US stocks today: Dow Jones crashes 500 points at open as Trump stops Iran peace deal
news.description: Wall Street opened lower after Trump said the Iran deal was over, triggering a risk-off sentiment globally. Rising oil prices added pressure on equities, with Dow, S&P 500 and Nasdaq slipping at the open as investors reacted to geopolitical uncertainty and potential inflation risks.

news.title: India's 10-year bond logs worst day in 3 months on faltering Iran deal
news.description: India's benchmark bond faced a dramatic decline, its most significant drop in over three months, primarily driven by a surge in oil prices linked to geopolitical tensions. news.title: Sebi amends rule for foreign investors, directs registration fee payment in Indian rupee
news.description: Sebi has announced a modification to its foreign investor registration fees, shifting to a rupee-based model effective in six months. news.title: NBFC credit grows 14% in May, led by retail and farm loans
news.description: Loans against gold jewellery remained the fastest-growing segment, surging 70% year-on-year to ₹3.29 lakh crore in May, compared with 39% growth a year earlier.
